title: Web App Development
instructions: Create a web application with user authentication (login) and a dashboard for logged‑in users.

1779830478.4746377.instruction: Use the TreeView XML tool to display the current project directory structure starting from the root, with a depth of 2, to understand the layout and locate key files (src/pca.sh, lok.sh, SYD/justtest.sh, SYSTEM/mirdir.sh).

1779830478.4783883.instruction: Review the contents of src/pca.sh, lok.sh, and SYD/justtest.sh to identify the variables, conventions, and entry points that the lok framework expects from integrated scripts.

1779830478.4987772.instruction: Open and examine the existing SYSTEM/mirdir.sh file to understand its current functionality and how it might be adapted to meet the lok framework's variable and naming requirements.

1779830478.50157.instruction: Edit mirdir.sh so that it defines and exports the variables required by the lok framework (e.g., LOK_PROJECT_ROOT, LOK_SCRIPT_DIR, etc.), adopts the same function naming conventions, and sources the core framework scripts appropriately.

1779830478.5040662.instruction: Run SYD/justtest.sh and src/pca.sh after the modification to ensure that mirdir.sh now works correctly within the lok framework and that all necessary environment variables are set as expected.

1779837572.8641772.instruction: Create a project folder with the following files: index.html, style.css, app.js, server.py. Ensure the folder is properly organized for front‑end and back‑end code.

1779837572.8697119.instruction: Implement server.py using Flask (or FastAPI). Add a POST endpoint /api/login that accepts JSON with username and password, validates them, and returns a JWT token. Add a GET endpoint /api/dashboard that requires a valid JWT token and returns user data.

1779837572.8737776.instruction: Create index.html containing a login form. Use JavaScript to send credentials to /api/login, store the returned JWT token in localStorage, and redirect to a dashboard page on success.

1779837572.8914816.instruction: Create a dashboard view that fetches user data from /api/dashboard using the stored JWT token, displays user information, and provides a logout button that clears the token.
